file.content:
# Review: deepmind/1109.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd≈ës Problem 1109`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/1109`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (largest subset with squarefree sumset, whether it is bounded by a polylogarithmic function).
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[ErSa87]` (Erd≈ës‚ÄìS√°rk√∂zy 1987) and `[Ko04]` (Konyagin 2004).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1109 ... end Erdos1109`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos1109`.
- [x] `open Finset Pointwise Real` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

N/A ‚Äî no custom `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_1109` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib snake_case conventions.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 5 11]`.
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`) describing the problem and known bounds.
- [x] Docstring references source papers: `[ErSa87]`, `[Ko04]`.

### `answer()` usage

The English problem asks "Is it true that f(N) ‚â§ (log N)^{O(1)}?" ‚Äî this is a **yes/no question** (Case 1).

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ‚Üî P`, correctly using Case 1 form.
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question as originally asked (existence of constants C > 0, k > 0 such that |A| ‚â§ C ¬∑ (log N)^k for all large N and all squarefree-sumset subsets A ‚äÜ {1,‚Ä¶,N}).
- [x] `answer(sorry)` is correct for an open problem (not yet solved).
- [x] Quantifiers (`‚àÉ C`, `‚àÉ k`, `‚àÉ N‚ÇÄ`, `‚àÄ N`, `‚àÄ A`) are **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research open` ‚Äî a valid category.
- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 5 and 11).
- [x] Numbers are in **ascending numeric order**: 5 < 11.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both 5 (combinatorics) and 11 (number theory) are valid AMS MSC2020 codes.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses multi-line `by` / `sorry` format (acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in any `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` (none present).
- [x] No informal proof sketches included.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

N/A ‚Äî no variants present. The file contains a single main theorem.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `‚àÄ`, `‚àÉ`, `‚àà`, `‚â§`, `‚àß`, `‚Üî`, `‚Ñù`, `‚Ñï`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all < 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` ‚Äî not verified in this review (requires build environment).
- [ ] Custom linters ‚Äî not verified in this review (requires build environment).

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (all checkable items pass)

The file is clean and fully conformant. The formalization correctly captures the yes/no question ("Is it true that f(N) ‚â§ (log N)^{O(1)}?") using `answer(sorry) ‚Üî P` with all quantifiers on the RHS. The docstring is thorough, citing both the original Erd≈ës‚ÄìS√°rk√∂zy paper and Konyagin's improvement, and clearly describing the known bounds. No issues found.

content.text: The review passed (**Result: PASS**). No changes needed.
